name: TORO CO
business: Toro designs, manufactures, markets, and sells professional turf maintenance equipment, irrigation systems, landscaping equipment, snow/ice management products, agricultural irrigation systems, rental/specialty/underground construction equipment, and residential yard/snow thrower products. Products are sold worldwide through distributors, dealers, mass retailers, hardware retailers, home centers, online, and direct to end-users under brands including Toro, Ditch Witch, eXmark, Spartan, BOSS, Ventrac, American Augers, Subsite, HammerHead, Radius, Perrot, Hayter, Unique Lighting Systems, Irritrol, and Lawn-Boy. The company operates in two segments: Professional (80.3% of net sales) and Residential (19.0%).
sellsTo: Commercial and residential irrigation and lighting

exposures.sentence: Equipment products for the golf market include large reel and rotary riding mowers for fairway, rough, and trim cutting, including autonomous solutions;
These irrigation products predominantly consist of sprinkler heads, controllers, turf sensors, valves and operating software.

We design, manufacture, market, and sell a range of professional grade products to serve the underground construction market under the Ditch Witch, American Augers, HammerHead, Subsite, and Radius brands, including horizontal directional drills, walk and ride trenchers, vacuum excavators, horizontal directional drilling guidance and support equipment, utility locators, utility inspection systems, pipe rehabilitation and replacement solutions, as well as after-market tools, including drive chucks and sub savers, drill pipe, starter rods and quick connects, bits and blades, rock tools, reamers, and swivels.

Irrigation products for the ag-irrigation market are designed, manufactured, marketed, and sold under the Toro brand and are intended to provide an efficient means of water application and usage in agricultural and greenhouse applications.

We market and sell our Residential segment products to homeowners through a variety of distribution channels, including outdoor power equipment distributors and dealers, mass retailers, hardware retailers, and home centers, as well as online and direct to end-users.

Our products are marketed and sold worldwide through a network of distributors, dealers, mass retailers, hardware retailers, equipment rental centers, and home centers, as well as online and direct to end-users under the primary trademarks of Toro, Ditch Witch, eXmark, Spartan, BOSS, Ventrac, American Augers, Subsite, HammerHead, Radius, Perrot, Hayter, Unique Lighting Systems, Irritrol, and Lawn-Boy, most of which are registered in the United States ("U.S.") and/or in the primary countries outside the U.S. where we market our products branded under such trademarks.
Such products are utilized by specialty contractors worldwide to install water, gas, electric, telecommunication, fiber optic, broadband, and other utility distribution systems.
Information technology security threats have been increasing in frequency and sophistication.

Our primary cost exposures for such items used in our products are with steel, aluminum, petroleum and natural gas-based resins, linerboard, copper, lead, rubber, engines, transmissions, transaxles, hydraulics, electrification components, and others, all of which we purchase from several suppliers around the world.
Our manufacturing operations include robotic and computer-automated equipment intended to speed production, reduce costs, and improve resource use and the quality, fit, and finish of our products.
Intelli360 for telematics and fleet management.
These irrigation products are supported by software, artificial intelligence, and apps to provide innovative water application solutions for both commercial and residential landscapes.

Equipment products for the landscape contractor market include gas and battery powered zero-turn radius riding mowers, heavy-duty and mid-size walk behind mowers including autonomous versions, gas and battery powered stand-on mowers, and manual and remote controlled all-wheel drive articulating tractors, as well as turf application, turf renovation, and tree care equipment.
For example, we have the risks related to developing and marketing electric and alternative fuel products to meet market demands for less greenhouse gas intensive products.

catalysts.event: AMP initiative targeted $125 million run-rate savings by fiscal 2027
catalysts.sentence: In the first quarter of fiscal 2024, we launched a significant productivity initiative named AMP, which is a multi-year initiative now on track to achieve at least $125 million of run-rate savings by fiscal 2027, up from the initial program estimate of at least $100 million.

catalysts.event: Expect backlog to normalize by mid-fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: Although we continue to see sustained demand for underground construction and golf and grounds products, we expect backlog to normalize by mid-fiscal 2026 given our improved manufacturing output and lower lead times.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of Tornado Infrastructure Equipment Ltd. on December 8, 2025
catalysts.sentence: On December 8, 2025, we acquired Tornado Infrastructure Equipment Ltd., a Calgary, Alberta, Canada-based manufacturer in the hydrovac excavation solutions industry for a purchase price of $279.3 million Canadian dollars.

catalysts.event: Quarterly dividend increased to $0.39 per share starting fiscal 2026 first quarter
catalysts.sentence: On December 9, 2025, our Board of Directors increased our fiscal 2026 first quarter common stock cash dividend by 2.6 percent to $0.39 per share.

catalysts.event: Board authorized additional 6,000,000 share repurchase on December 9, 2025
catalysts.sentence: On December 9, 2025, the company's Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to an additional 6,000,000 shares of common stock under the stock repurchase program.
